六所明神社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} *[[黒板勝美]] 國史大系編修会 編 『[[国史大系|國史大系]] 第32巻 [[吾妻鑑]]前編』 [[吉川弘文館]] 1964年7月 *黒板勝美 國史大系編修会 編 『國史大系 第33巻 吾妻鑑後編』 吉川弘文館 1965年2月 *全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター 編 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』 全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター 1977年7月 *神道大系編纂会編 『神道大系 神社編16 駿河・伊豆・甲斐・相模国』 神道大系編纂会 1980年3月 *杉山博 下山治久 編 『戦国遺文 後北条氏編 第1巻』 [[東京堂出版]] 1989年9月 *中世諸国一宮制研究会編 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 岩田書院 2000年2月 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} * [http://www.rokusho.jp/ 相模国総社 六所神社] - 公式サイト {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ろくしよしんしや おおいそ}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:相模国|社0]] [[Category:総社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 六所神社 |画像 = [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), haiden-from afar.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷935 |位置 = {{Coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E|region:JP-14_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[櫛稲田姫命]]
[[須佐之男命]]
[[大己貴尊]]
寒川神社祭神
川勾神社祭神
比々多神社祭神
前鳥神社祭神
平塚八幡宮祭神 |社格 = [[相模国]][[総社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 第10代[[崇神天皇]]甲申年 |本殿 = 五間社[[流造]] |別名 = 柳田大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月第1日曜日 |神事 = 国府祭 （[[5月5日]]） |地図 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|鳥居}}]] [[ファイル:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|本殿}}{{small|造営当時は全体が[[朱塗り]]であった。相模五社の神々を祭るため5つの扉がある。『快元僧都記』によれば天文9年（[[1540年]]）[[鶴岡八幡宮]]造営の参考にするため当社[[棟札]]を持ち出したと言い、[[平成]]の修築では棟札が見つけられなかった。}}]] [[ファイル:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|神揃山}}{{small|道路の突き当りに見えるのが[[国府祭]]で[[座問答神事]]が行われる神揃山。馬場公園付近より撮影。}}]] '''六所神社'''（ろくしょじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷にある[[神社]]。[[相模国]][[総社]]で{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 毎年5月5日に往古[[神領]]の神揃山（神集山）で[[国府祭]]（こうのまち）を催行し、相模国の[[一宮]]から[[四宮]]および[[平塚八幡宮]]（[[五宮]]格）の[[神輿渡御]]を受ける。 == 祭神 == 当地に元々あった柳田大明神社に、相模国の[[一宮]]から四宮まで、および五宮格の平塚八幡宮の[[祭神]]を[[合祀]]しており、六社の神を祀ることから「六所神社」という。 * 柳田大明神 -- [[クシナダヒメ|櫛稲田姫命]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[大国主|大己貴尊]] * 一宮：[[寒川神社]]祭神 * 二宮：[[川勾神社]]祭神 * 三宮：[[比々多神社]]祭神 * 四宮：[[前鳥神社]]祭神 * [[平塚八幡宮]]祭神 == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の時代、[[出雲国]]（現在の[[島根県]]東部）より移住しこの地を開墾した人々は、この地を[[柳田郷]]と名附け、出雲の祖神である櫛稲田姫命・須佐之男命・大己貴尊を祀って「柳田大明神」と称し、現在地より北西1kmの石上台（伊勢神台）に社殿を築いた。『六所明神之縁起』『六所明神之縁起』は大磯町国府新宿の蓮華院が所蔵する当社の[[縁起]]（神道大系編纂会編 『神道大系 神社編16 駿河・伊豆・甲斐・相模国』 神道大系編纂会 1980年3月 に所収）。によれば、創建の年は崇神天皇[[甲申]]年崇神天皇元年（[[594年]]）または61年（[[624年]]）であると考えられる。『六所明神之縁起』では庚申年の箇所に「元年ヵ」と割注がある。と伝えられる。 [[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）[[元正天皇]]より[[国司]]に対し、勅を以って当社を相模国の神祇の中心として総社に定めるという宣下がなされ、これにより同年閏4月8日養老2年（718年）に閏4月は無い。『六所明神之縁起』ではこの箇所に「六ヵ」と割注があり、正しくは養老6年（[[722年]]）閏4月8日ではないかと考察している。に現在地へ遷座した。[[平安時代]]には[[国府]]に近い当社に国内有力5社の祭神の分霊を[[勧請]]し、国府六所宮とも称されるようになった。また社伝によれば、[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）5月5日の[[早良親王]]夷賊征討に際し退攘祈願が行われたとしている。 『[[吾妻鏡]]』にみられる当社に関連する記事を、時系列的に並べる。 *[[治承]]4年（[[1180年]]）10月16日の条：[[源頼朝]]が[[富士川の戦い]]に向かう途中で「相模国府六所宮」に到り、戦勝を祈願したとされる。 *治承4年（1180年）10月23日の条：相模国府において[[北条時政]]等25人へ論功行賞が行われた記事があるが、これは当社神領において行われたと伝えられる。 *[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）11月24日の条：当社を含む、相模国内の仏寺15ヶ所と神社11ヶ所へ国土太平の御願書を奉った記事がある。 *文治2年（[[1186年]]）5月29日の条：源頼朝が[[東海道]]の総社ならびに[[国分寺]]の復興を命じた際に、『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』中世諸国一宮制研究会編 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 岩田書院 2000年2月 より。によると、当社も修復を受けた可能性が高い。 *[[建久]]3年（[[1192年]]）5月8日の条：[[後白河天皇|後白河法皇]]の[[中陰|七七日]]仏事へ参加した僧衆の中に「六所宮二口」が見えることから、当社より供僧2名が参加したことがわかる。また、この際に御礼として白布3段と米1袋を頂いている。 *建久3年（1192年）8月9日の条：源頼朝が[[北条政子]]の安産を祈願した神社として「総社柳田」の名が見え、この時に[[神馬]]が奉納された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 *[[建長]]4年（[[1252年]]）4月14日の条：[[宗尊親王]]が鎌倉に下向された際、将軍事始の儀として、鶴岡八幡宮以下の、当社を含む大社に神馬を奉納した記事がある{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国期]]に入り、[[永正|永正年間]]（[[1504年]] - [[1520年]]）に[[北条氏綱]]が社殿を造営、『六所明神之縁起』によれば[[天正|天正年間]]（[[1573年]] - [[1593年]]）に[[北条氏政]]が本殿を修復し、現在の本殿ができあがった。また拝殿基壇の石垣も[[後北条氏]]の寄進によるものである。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]13年（[[1544年]]）12月23日付けの『北条家朱印状』戦国遺文 後北条氏編258号。杉山博 下山治久 編 『戦国遺文 後北条氏編 第1巻』 東京堂出版 1989年9月 に所収。では65[[貫]]78[[文 (通貨単位)|文]]の[[知行]]高を安堵し、さらに造営方として30貫500文の追加が認められており、後北条家から厚く保護されたのが分かる。 [[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）[[徳川家康]]より[[朱印状|御朱印状]]が下され、[[朱印地・黒印地|御朱印地]]50[[石 (単位)|石]]が寄進された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。『六所明神之縁起』によれば、以後代々の将軍家からも寄進を受けたと言う。『[[新編相模国風土記稿]] 巻之40』蘆田伊人 編 『大日本地誌大系15 新編相模国風土記稿第2巻』 雄山閣 1962年9月 より。『新編相模国風土記稿』は[[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）の成立。によれば、[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）4月に府中祭礼における狼藉や喧嘩の禁止、社殿修築における[[百姓]]の助成などを定めた制札が[[江戸幕府]]より出され、保護を受けている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[明治]]の[[近代社格制度]]では郷社へ列格された。 ==祭事== * [[元旦祭]]（1月1日） *[[節分]]祭（2月3日） * [[国府祭]]（5月5日） * [[例大祭]]（櫛魂祭、別称：くしまつり）（9月4日前後の日曜日） * [[放生会]]（11月23日） * 大祓式（12月中） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' '''出典''' {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[蘆田伊人]] 編 『[[大日本地誌大系]]15 [[新編相模国風土記稿]]第2巻』 [[雄山閣]] 1962年9月 * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻新編相模国風土記稿2巻之40村里部淘綾郡巻之2|chapter=二ノ宮庄 国府新宿 六所明神社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/156}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} *[[黒板勝美]] 國史大系編修会 編 『[[国史大系|國史大系]] 第32巻 [[吾妻鑑]]前編』 [[吉川弘文館]] 1964年7月 *黒板勝美 國史大系編修会 編 『國史大系 第33巻 吾妻鑑後編』 吉川弘文館 1965年2月 *全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター 編 『全国神社名鑑 上巻』 全国神社名鑑刊行会史学センター 1977年7月 *神道大系編纂会編 『神道大系 神社編16 駿河・伊豆・甲斐・相模国』 神道大系編纂会 1980年3月 *杉山博 下山治久 編 『戦国遺文 後北条氏編 第1巻』 [[東京堂出版]] 1989年9月 *中世諸国一宮制研究会編 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 岩田書院 2000年2月 == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} * {{神社 |名称 = 六所神社 |画像 = [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), haiden-from afar.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷935 |位置 = {{Coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E|region:JP-14_scale:10000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[櫛稲田姫命]]
[[須佐之男命]]
[[大己貴尊]]
寒川神社祭神
川勾神社祭神
比々多神社祭神
前鳥神社祭神
平塚八幡宮祭神 |社格 = [[相模国]][[総社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 第10代[[崇神天皇]]甲申年 |本殿 = 五間社[[流造]] |別名 = 柳田大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月第1日曜日 |神事 = 国府祭 （[[5月5日]]） |地図 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|鳥居}}]] [[ファイル:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|本殿}}{{small|造営当時は全体が[[朱塗り]]であった。相模五社の神々を祭るため5つの扉がある。『快元僧都記』によれば天文9年（[[1540年]]）[[鶴岡八幡宮]]造営の参考にするため当社[[棟札]]を持ち出したと言い、[[平成]]の修築では棟札が見つけられなかった。}}]] [[ファイル:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|神揃山}}{{small|道路の突き当りに見えるのが[[国府祭]]で[[座問答神事]]が行われる神揃山。馬場公園付近より撮影。}}]] '''六所神社'''（ろくしょじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷にある[[神社]]。[[相模国]][[総社]]で{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 毎年5月5日に往古[[神領]]の神揃山（神集山）で[[国府祭]]（こうのまち）を催行し、相模国の[[一宮]]から[[四宮]]および[[平塚八幡宮]]（[[五宮]]格）の[[神輿渡御]]を受ける。 == 祭神 == 当地に元々あった柳田大明神社に、相模国の[[一宮]]から四宮まで、および五宮格の平塚八幡宮の[[祭神]]を[[合祀]]しており、六社の神を祀ることから「六所神社」という。 * 柳田大明神 -- [[クシナダヒメ|櫛稲田姫命]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[大国主|大己貴尊]] * 一宮：[[寒川神社]]祭神 * 二宮：[[川勾神社]]祭神 * 三宮：[[比々多神社]]祭神 * 四宮：[[前鳥神社]]祭神 * [[平塚八幡宮]]祭神 == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の時代、[[出雲国]]（現在の[[島根県]]東部）より移住しこの地を開墾した人々は、この地を[[柳田郷]]と名附け、出雲の祖神である櫛稲田姫命・須佐之男命・大己貴尊を祀って「柳田大明神」と称し、現在地より北西1kmの石上台（伊勢神台）に社殿を築いた。『六所明神之縁起』『六所明神之縁起』は大磯町国府新宿の蓮華院が所蔵する当社の[[縁起]]（神道大系編纂会編 『神道大系 神社編16 駿河・伊豆・甲斐・相模国』 神道大系編纂会 1980年3月 に所収）。によれば、創建の年は崇神天皇[[甲申]]年崇神天皇元年（[[594年]]）または61年（[[624年]]）であると考えられる。『六所明神之縁起』では庚申年の箇所に「元年ヵ」と割注がある。と伝えられる。 [[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）[[元正天皇]]より[[国司]]に対し、勅を以って当社を相模国の神祇の中心として総社に定めるという宣下がなされ、これにより同年閏4月8日養老2年（718年）に閏4月は無い。『六所明神之縁起』ではこの箇所に「六ヵ」と割注があり、正しくは養老6年（[[722年]]）閏4月8日ではないかと考察している。に現在地へ遷座した。[[平安時代]]には[[国府]]に近い当社に国内有力5社の祭神の分霊を[[勧請]]し、国府六所宮とも称されるようになった。また社伝によれば、[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）5月5日の[[早良親王]]夷賊征討に際し退攘祈願が行われたとしている。 『[[吾妻鏡]]』にみられる当社に関連する記事を、時系列的に並べる。 *[[治承]]4年（[[1180年]]）10月16日の条：[[源頼朝]]が[[富士川の戦い]]に向かう途中で「相模国府六所宮」に到り、戦勝を祈願したとされる。 *治承4年（1180年）10月23日の条：相模国府において[[北条時政]]等25人へ論功行賞が行われた記事があるが、これは当社神領において行われたと伝えられる。 *[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）11月24日の条：当社を含む、相模国内の仏寺15ヶ所と神社11ヶ所へ国土太平の御願書を奉った記事がある。 *文治2年（[[1186年]]）5月29日の条：源頼朝が[[東海道]]の総社ならびに[[国分寺]]の復興を命じた際に、『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』中世諸国一宮制研究会編 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 岩田書院 2000年2月 より。によると、当社も修復を受けた可能性が高い。 *[[建久]]3年（[[1192年]]）5月8日の条：[[後白河天皇|後白河法皇]]の[[中陰|七七日]]仏事へ参加した僧衆の中に「六所宮二口」が見えることから、当社より供僧2名が参加したことがわかる。また、この際に御礼として白布3段と米1袋を頂いている。 *建久3年（1192年）8月9日の条：源頼朝が[[北条政子]]の安産を祈願した神社として「総社柳田」の名が見え、この時に[[神馬]]が奉納された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 *[[建長]]4年（[[1252年]]）4月14日の条：[[宗尊親王]]が鎌倉に下向された際、将軍事始の儀として、鶴岡八幡宮以下の、当社を含む大社に神馬を奉納した記事がある{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国期]]に入り、[[永正|永正年間]]（[[1504年]] - [[1520年]]）に[[北条氏綱]]が社殿を造営、『六所明神之縁起』によれば[[天正|天正年間]]（[[1573年]] - [[1593年]]）に[[北条氏政]]が本殿を修復し、現在の本殿ができあがった。また拝殿基壇の石垣も[[後北条氏]]の寄進によるものである。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]13年（[[1544年]]）12月23日付けの『北条家朱印状』戦国遺文 後北条氏編258号。杉山博 下山治久 編 『戦国遺文 後北条氏編 第1巻』 東京堂出版 1989年9月 に所収。では65[[貫]]78[[文 (通貨単位)|文]]の[[知行]]高を安堵し、さらに造営方として30貫500文の追加が認められており、後北条家から厚く保護されたのが分かる。 [[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）[[徳川家康]]より[[朱印状|御朱印状]]が下され、[[朱印地・黒印地|御朱印地]]50[[石 (単位)|石]]が寄進された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。『六所明神之縁起』によれば、以後代々の将軍家からも寄進を受けたと言う。『[[新編相模国風土記稿]] 巻之40』蘆田伊人 編 『大日本地誌大系15 新編相模国風土記稿第2巻』 雄山閣 1962年9月 より。『新編相模国風土記稿』は[[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）の成立。によれば、[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）4月に府中祭礼における狼藉や喧嘩の禁止、社殿修築における[[百姓]]の助成などを定めた制札が[[江戸幕府]]より出され、保護を受けている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[明治]]の[[近代社格制度]]では郷社へ列格された。 ==祭事== * [[元旦祭]]（1月1日） *[[節分]]祭（2月3日） * [[国府祭]]（5月5日） * [[例大祭]]（櫛魂祭、別称：くしまつり）（9月4日前後の日曜日） * [[放生会]]（11月23日） * 大祓式（12月中） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' '''出典''' {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[蘆田伊人]] 編 『[[大日本地誌大系]]15 [[新編相模国風土記稿]]第2巻』 [[雄山閣]] 1962年9月 * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻新編相模国風土記稿2巻之40村里部淘綾郡巻之2|chapter=二ノ宮庄 国府新宿 六所明神社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/156}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|本殿}}{{small|造営当時は全体が[[朱塗り]]であった。相模五社の神々を祭るため5つの扉がある。『快元僧都記』によれば天文9年（[[1540年]]）[[鶴岡八幡宮]]造営の参考にするため当社[[棟札]]を持ち出したと言い、[[平成]]の修築では棟札が見つけられなかった。}}]] [[ファイル:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|神揃山}}{{small|道路の突き当りに見えるのが[[国府祭]]で[[座問答神事]]が行われる神揃山。馬場公園付近より撮影。}}]] '''六所神社'''（ろくしょじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷にある[[神社]]。[[相模国]][[総社]]で{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 毎年5月5日に往古[[神領]]の神揃山（神集山）で[[国府祭]]（こうのまち）を催行し、相模国の[[一宮]]から[[四宮]]および[[平塚八幡宮]]（[[五宮]]格）の[[神輿渡御]]を受ける。 == 祭神 == 当地に元々あった柳田大明神社に、相模国の[[一宮]]から四宮まで、および五宮格の平塚八幡宮の[[祭神]]を[[合祀]]しており、六社の神を祀ることから「六所神社」という。 * 柳田大明神 -- [[クシナダヒメ|櫛稲田姫命]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[大国主|大己貴尊]] * 一宮：[[寒川神社]]祭神 * 二宮：[[川勾神社]]祭神 * 三宮：[[比々多神社]]祭神 * 四宮：[[前鳥神社]]祭神 * [[平塚八幡宮]]祭神 == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の時代、[[出雲国]]（現在の[[島根県]]東部）より移住しこの地を開墾した人々は、この地を[[柳田郷]]と名附け、出雲の祖神である櫛稲田姫命・須佐之男命・大己貴尊を祀って「柳田大明神」と称し、現在地より北西1kmの石上台（伊勢神台）に社殿を築いた。『六所明神之縁起』『六所明神之縁起』は大磯町国府新宿の蓮華院が所蔵する当社の[[縁起]]（神道大系編纂会編 『神道大系 神社編16 駿河・伊豆・甲斐・相模国』 神道大系編纂会 1980年3月 に所収）。によれば、創建の年は崇神天皇[[甲申]]年崇神天皇元年（[[594年]]）または61年（[[624年]]）であると考えられる。『六所明神之縁起』では庚申年の箇所に「元年ヵ」と割注がある。と伝えられる。 [[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）[[元正天皇]]より[[国司]]に対し、勅を以って当社を相模国の神祇の中心として総社に定めるという宣下がなされ、これにより同年閏4月8日養老2年（718年）に閏4月は無い。『六所明神之縁起』ではこの箇所に「六ヵ」と割注があり、正しくは養老6年（[[722年]]）閏4月8日ではないかと考察している。に現在地へ遷座した。[[平安時代]]には[[国府]]に近い当社に国内有力5社の祭神の分霊を[[勧請]]し、国府六所宮とも称されるようになった。また社伝によれば、[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）5月5日の[[早良親王]]夷賊征討に際し退攘祈願が行われたとしている。 『[[吾妻鏡]]』にみられる当社に関連する記事を、時系列的に並べる。 *[[治承]]4年（[[1180年]]）10月16日の条：[[源頼朝]]が[[富士川の戦い]]に向かう途中で「相模国府六所宮」に到り、戦勝を祈願したとされる。 *治承4年（1180年）10月23日の条：相模国府において[[北条時政]]等25人へ論功行賞が行われた記事があるが、これは当社神領において行われたと伝えられる。 *[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）11月24日の条：当社を含む、相模国内の仏寺15ヶ所と神社11ヶ所へ国土太平の御願書を奉った記事がある。 *文治2年（[[1186年]]）5月29日の条：源頼朝が[[Tōkaidō (region)|Tōkaidō]]の総社ならびに[[国分寺]]の復興を命じた際に、『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』中世諸国一宮制研究会編 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 岩田書院 2000年2月 より。によると、当社も修復を受けた可能性が高い。 *[[建久]]3年（[[1192年]]）5月8日の条：[[後白河天皇|後白河法皇]]の[[中陰|七七日]]仏事へ参加した僧衆の中に「六所宮二口」が見えることから、当社より供僧2名が参加したことがわかる。また、この際に御礼として白布3段と米1袋を頂いている。 *建久3年（1192年）8月9日の条：源頼朝が[[北条政子]]の安産を祈願した神社として「総社柳田」の名が見え、この時に[[神馬]]が奉納された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 *[[建長]]4年（[[1252年]]）4月14日の条：[[宗尊親王]]が鎌倉に下向された際、将軍事始の儀として、鶴岡八幡宮以下の、当社を含む大社に神馬を奉納した記事がある{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国期]]に入り、[[永正|永正年間]]（[[1504年]] - [[1520年]]）に[[北条氏綱]]が社殿を造営、『六所明神之縁起』によれば[[天正|天正年間]]（[[1573年]] - [[1593年]]）に[[北条氏政]]が本殿を修復し、現在の本殿ができあがった。また拝殿基壇の石垣も[[後北条氏]]の寄進によるものである。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]13年（[[1544年]]）12月23日付けの『北条家朱印状』戦国遺文 後北条氏編258号。杉山博 下山治久 編 『戦国遺文 後北条氏編 第1巻』 東京堂出版 1989年9月 に所収。では65[[貫]]78[[文 (通貨単位)|文]]の[[知行]]高を安堵し、さらに造営方として30貫500文の追加が認められており、後北条家から厚く保護されたのが分かる。 [[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）[[徳川家康]]より[[朱印状|御朱印状]]が下され、[[朱印地・黒印地|御朱印地]]50[[石 (単位)|石]]が寄進された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。『六所明神之縁起』によれば、以後代々の将軍家からも寄進を受けたと言う。『[[新編相模国風土記稿]] 巻之40』蘆田伊人 編 『大日本地誌大系15 新編相模国風土記稿第2巻』 雄山閣 1962年9月 より。『新編相模国風土記稿』は[[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）の成立。によれば、[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）4月に府中祭礼における狼藉や喧嘩の禁止、社殿修築における[[百姓]]の助成などを定めた制札が[[江戸幕府]]より出され、保護を受けている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[明治]]の[[近代社格制度]]では郷社へ列格された。 ==祭事== * '''六所神社'''（ろくしょじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]国府本郷にある[[神社]]。[[相模国]][[総社]]で{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 毎年5月5日に往古[[神領]]の神揃山（神集山）で[[国府祭]]（こうのまち）を催行し、相模国の[[一宮]]から[[四宮]]および[[平塚八幡宮]]（[[五宮]]格）の[[神輿渡御]]を受ける。 == 祭神 == 当地に元々あった柳田大明神社に、相模国の[[一宮]]から四宮まで、および五宮格の平塚八幡宮の[[祭神]]を[[合祀]]しており、六社の神を祀ることから「六所神社」という。 * 柳田大明神 -- [[クシナダヒメ|櫛稲田姫命]]・[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]・[[大国主|大己貴尊]] * 一宮：[[寒川神社]]祭神 * 二宮：[[川勾神社]]祭神 * 三宮：[[比々多神社]]祭神 * 四宮：[[前鳥神社]]祭神 * [[平塚八幡宮]]祭神 == 歴史 == [[崇神天皇]]の時代、[[出雲国]]（現在の[[島根県]]東部）より移住しこの地を開墾した人々は、この地を[[柳田郷]]と名附け、出雲の祖神である櫛稲田姫命・須佐之男命・大己貴尊を祀って「柳田大明神」と称し、現在地より北西1kmの石上台（伊勢神台）に社殿を築いた。『六所明神之縁起』『六所明神之縁起』は大磯町国府新宿の蓮華院が所蔵する当社の[[縁起]]（神道大系編纂会編 『神道大系 神社編16 駿河・伊豆・甲斐・相模国』 神道大系編纂会 1980年3月 に所収）。によれば、創建の年は崇神天皇[[甲申]]年崇神天皇元年（[[594年]]）または61年（[[624年]]）であると考えられる。『六所明神之縁起』では庚申年の箇所に「元年ヵ」と割注がある。と伝えられる。 [[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）[[元正天皇]]より[[国司]]に対し、勅を以って当社を相模国の神祇の中心として総社に定めるという宣下がなされ、これにより同年閏4月8日養老2年（718年）に閏4月は無い。『六所明神之縁起』ではこの箇所に「六ヵ」と割注があり、正しくは養老6年（[[722年]]）閏4月8日ではないかと考察している。に現在地へ遷座した。[[平安時代]]には[[国府]]に近い当社に国内有力5社の祭神の分霊を[[勧請]]し、国府六所宮とも称されるようになった。また社伝によれば、[[天応 (日本)|天応]]元年（[[781年]]）5月5日の[[早良親王]]夷賊征討に際し退攘祈願が行われたとしている。 『[[吾妻鏡]]』にみられる当社に関連する記事を、時系列的に並べる。 *[[治承]]4年（[[1180年]]）10月16日の条：[[源頼朝]]が[[富士川の戦い]]に向かう途中で「相模国府六所宮」に到り、戦勝を祈願したとされる。 *治承4年（1180年）10月23日の条：相模国府において[[北条時政]]等25人へ論功行賞が行われた記事があるが、これは当社神領において行われたと伝えられる。 *[[文治]]元年（[[1185年]]）11月24日の条：当社を含む、相模国内の仏寺15ヶ所と神社11ヶ所へ国土太平の御願書を奉った記事がある。 *文治2年（[[1186年]]）5月29日の条：源頼朝が[[Tōkaidō (region)|Tōkaidō]]の総社ならびに[[国分寺]]の復興を命じた際に、『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』中世諸国一宮制研究会編 『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 岩田書院 2000年2月 より。によると、当社も修復を受けた可能性が高い。 *[[建久]]3年（[[1192年]]）5月8日の条：[[後白河天皇|後白河法皇]]の[[中陰|七七日]]仏事へ参加した僧衆の中に「六所宮二口」が見えることから、当社より供僧2名が参加したことがわかる。また、この際に御礼として白布3段と米1袋を頂いている。 *建久3年（1192年）8月9日の条：源頼朝が[[北条政子]]の安産を祈願した神社として「総社柳田」の名が見え、この時に[[神馬]]が奉納された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 *[[建長]]4年（[[1252年]]）4月14日の条：[[宗尊親王]]が鎌倉に下向された際、将軍事始の儀として、鶴岡八幡宮以下の、当社を含む大社に神馬を奉納した記事がある{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国期]]に入り、[[永正|永正年間]]（[[1504年]] - [[1520年]]）に[[北条氏綱]]が社殿を造営、『六所明神之縁起』によれば[[天正|天正年間]]（[[1573年]] - [[1593年]]）に[[北条氏政]]が本殿を修復し、現在の本殿ができあがった。また拝殿基壇の石垣も[[後北条氏]]の寄進によるものである。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]13年（[[1544年]]）12月23日付けの『北条家朱印状』戦国遺文 後北条氏編258号。杉山博 下山治久 編 『戦国遺文 後北条氏編 第1巻』 東京堂出版 1989年9月 に所収。では65[[貫]]78[[文 (通貨単位)|文]]の[[知行]]高を安堵し、さらに造営方として30貫500文の追加が認められており、後北条家から厚く保護されたのが分かる。 [[天正]]19年（[[1591年]]）[[徳川家康]]より[[朱印状|御朱印状]]が下され、[[朱印地・黒印地|御朱印地]]50[[石 (単位)|石]]が寄進された{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。『六所明神之縁起』によれば、以後代々の将軍家からも寄進を受けたと言う。『[[新編相模国風土記稿]] 巻之40』蘆田伊人 編 『大日本地誌大系15 新編相模国風土記稿第2巻』 雄山閣 1962年9月 より。『新編相模国風土記稿』は[[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）の成立。によれば、[[正保]]4年（[[1647年]]）4月に府中祭礼における狼藉や喧嘩の禁止、社殿修築における[[百姓]]の助成などを定めた制札が[[江戸幕府]]より出され、保護を受けている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 国府新宿 六所明神社}}。 [[明治]]の[[近代社格制度]]では郷社へ列格された。 ==祭事== * There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540年}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594年}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624年}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|718|ja|718年}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722年}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応}} ({{ill|781|ja|781年}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180年}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185年}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{Sōja shrines}} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594年}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624年}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|718|ja|718年}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722年}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応}} ({{ill|781|ja|781年}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180年}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185年}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}} (May 5) }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540年}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594年}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624年}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|718|ja|718年}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year }}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year }}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180年}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185年}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year }}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year }}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|718|ja|718年}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year }}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year }}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180年}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year }}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{Sōja shrines}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540年}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事}} is held. revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name = Rokusho Shrine (六所神社) |image = [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), haiden-from afar.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡}}{{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町}} Kokuhongo 935 |Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命}}
{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊}}
{{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} enshrined deity |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}{{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}} (May 5) }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year }}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year }}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year }}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year }}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year }}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year }}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year }}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year }}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|12=draft}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=draft}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=draft}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|12=draft}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=draft}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|12=draft}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|12=draft}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|12=draft}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|12=draft}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=draft}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申|12=draft}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year|12=draft}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year|12=draft}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|12=draft}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|12=draft}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|12=draft}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|12=draft}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|12=draft}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|12=draft}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|12=draft}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|12=draft}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|12=draft}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|12=draft}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|12=draft}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|12=draft}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year|12=draft}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=draft}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=draft}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|12=draft}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|12=draft}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|12=draft}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|12=draft}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|12=draft}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|12=draft}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|12=draft}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|12=draft}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|12=draft}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|12=draft}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|12=draft}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|12=draft}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Sōja shrines}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name = Rokusho Shrine (六所神社) |image = [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), haiden-from afar.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}{{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|12=draft}} Kokuhongo 935 |Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|12=draft}}
{{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft}} enshrined deity |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft}}{{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft}} (May 5) }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り|12=draft}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year|12=draft}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=draft}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=draft}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|12=draft}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=draft}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=draft}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=draft}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|12=draft}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=draft}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|12=draft}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=draft}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申|12=draft}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year|12=draft}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year|12=draft}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|12=draft}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|12=draft}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|12=draft}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|12=draft}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|12=draft}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|12=draft}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|12=draft}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|12=draft}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|12=draft}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|12=draft}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|12=draft}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|12=draft}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|12=draft}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Sōja shrines}}

During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|12=draft|12=draft}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|12=draft|12=draft}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|12=draft|12=draft}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{draft categories| }} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine |Name = Rokusho Shrine (六所神社) |image = [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), haiden-from afar.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|12=draft|12=draft}} Kokuhongo 935 |Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 5) }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り|12=draft|12=draft}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=draft|12=draft}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft|12=draft}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=draft|12=draft}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft|12=draft}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|12=draft|12=draft}} its former rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=draft|12=draft}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=draft|12=draft}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=draft|12=draft}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft|12=draft}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft|12=draft}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=draft|12=draft}} rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|12=draft|12=draft}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft|12=draft}} (of Fifth Shrine rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|12=draft|12=draft}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|12=draft|12=draft}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|12=draft|12=draft}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|12=draft|12=draft}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=draft|12=draft}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申|12=draft|12=draft}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|12=draft|12=draft}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|12=draft|12=draft}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|12=draft|12=draft}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|12=draft|12=draft}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=draft|12=draft}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration {{draft categories| }} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]
