{{Shinto Shrine |Name = Rokusho Shrine (六所神社) |image = [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), haiden-from afar.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}{{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}} Kokuhongo 935 |Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}}{{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} (May 5) }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vermilion-painted|qq=}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1540|qq=}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|qq=}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|qq=}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|qq=}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|qq=}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|qq=}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|qq=}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|qq=}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/594|qq=}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/624|qq=}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/718|qq=}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Genshō|qq=}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Governor|qq=}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/722|qq=}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period|qq=}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Capital|qq=}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Invitation|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sarara|qq=}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|2=ja|3=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} (May 5) }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vermilion-painted|qq=}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1540|qq=}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|qq=}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|qq=}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|qq=}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|qq=}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|qq=}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|qq=}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|qq=}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/594|qq=}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/624|qq=}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/718|qq=}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Genshō|qq=}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Governor|qq=}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/722|qq=}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period|qq=}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Capital|qq=}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Invitation|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sarara|qq=}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|2=ja|3=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|qq=}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|qq=}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/594|qq=}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/624|qq=}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/718|qq=}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Genshō|qq=}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Governor|qq=}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/722|qq=}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period|qq=}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Capital|qq=}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Invitation|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sarara|qq=}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1540|qq=}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|qq=}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|qq=}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|qq=}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|qq=}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|qq=}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period|qq=}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Capital|qq=}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Invitation|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sarara|qq=}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

{{Shinto Shrine |Name = Rokusho Shrine (六所神社) |image = [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), haiden-from afar.jpg|280px]]
Worship Hall |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}{{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}} Kokuhongo 935 |Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
{{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}}{{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} (May 5) }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vermilion-painted|qq=}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1540|qq=}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|qq=}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|qq=}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|qq=}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|qq=}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|qq=}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|qq=}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|qq=}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/594|qq=}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/624|qq=}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} ({{ill|718|ja|718th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/718|qq=}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Genshō|qq=}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Governor|qq=}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/722|qq=}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period|qq=}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Capital|qq=}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Invitation|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sarara|qq=}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1540|qq=}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|qq=}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|qq=}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|qq=}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|qq=}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|qq=}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|qq=}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|qq=}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/594|qq=}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/624|qq=}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} ({{ill|718|ja|718年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/718|qq=}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Genshō|qq=}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Governor|qq=}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/722|qq=}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period|qq=}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Capital|qq=}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Invitation|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sarara|qq=}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|ja|寒川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Samukawa Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|qq=}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|qq=}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|qq=}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|qq=}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|ja|594年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/594|qq=}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|ja|624年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/624|qq=}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō|qq=}} ({{ill|718|ja|718年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/718|qq=}}), during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Genshō|qq=}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Provincial Governor|ja|国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Governor|qq=}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|ja|722年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/722|qq=}}).. During the {{ill|Heian Period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period|qq=}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Provincial Capital|ja|国府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Provincial Capital|qq=}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Enshrinement Invitation|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrinement Invitation|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjō|qq=}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|ja|天応 (日本)|lt=Ten'ō|lt_ja=天応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'ō|qq=}} ({{ill|781|ja|781st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/781|qq=}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sarara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sarara|qq=}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]

There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|ja|1540年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1540|qq=}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|qq=}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|qq=}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Oiso Town|ja|大磯町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oiso Town|qq=}}, {{ill|Naka District|ja|中郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka District|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja shrine|lt=Sōja Shrine|ja|総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Province|qq=}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|qq=}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|qq=}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|qq=}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|qq=}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|qq=}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|lt=Kushinadahime|lt_ja=櫛稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ・ In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|ja|1186年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1186|qq=}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō era|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō era|qq=}} ({{ill|1180|ja|1180年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1180|qq=}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of the Fuji River|ja|富士川の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of the Fuji River|qq=}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hojo Tokimasa|ja|北条時政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hojo Tokimasa|qq=}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji era|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunji era|qq=}} ({{ill|1185|ja|1185年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1185|qq=}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|qq=}} Sōja and the {{ill|Kokubun-ji|ja|国分寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubun-ji|qq=}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] }} [[sn:Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)]] [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]]
