According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ([[:en:1540|1540]]) the design of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine]] was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Kokuhongo, [[:en:Ōiso|Oiso Town]], [[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja Shrine]] of [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]],{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神幸祭|1=shinkō-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123907414}} is received from Sagami Province’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東山天皇|1=Higashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313801}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime]] ・ [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] ・ [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * Ichinomiya: [[:en:Samukawa Shrine|Samukawa Shrine]] enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], migrants from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] (present-day eastern [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ([[:en:594|594]]) or the 61st year ([[:en:624|624]]). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] ([[:en:718|718]]), during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Emperor Genshō]], an imperial decree was issued to the [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial Governor]] designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ([[:en:722|722]]).. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], our shrine near the provincial capital ([[:en:Kokufu|Provincial Capital]]) invited the spirit manifestations ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of [[:en:Ten'ō|Ten'ō]] ([[:en:781|781]]) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sarara]], exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ([[:en:1186|1186]]) on May 29, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubun-ji]], the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō era]] ([[:en:1180|1180]]) on October 16: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have, on his way to the [[:en:Battle of Fujigawa|Battle of the Fuji River]], arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hojo Tokimasa]] at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji era]] ([[:en:1185|1185]]) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubun-ji]], according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ([[:en:1540|1540]]) the design of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine]] was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Kokuhongo, [[:en:Ōiso|Oiso Town]], [[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Sōja shrine|Sōja Shrine]] of [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]],{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神幸祭|1=shinkō-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123907414}} is received from Sagami Province’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東山天皇|1=Higashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313801}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s [[:en:Ichinomiya|Ichinomiya]] through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – [[:en:Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime]] ・ [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] ・ [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] * Ichinomiya: [[:en:Samukawa Shrine|Samukawa Shrine]] enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], migrants from [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo Province]] (present-day eastern [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ([[:en:594|594]]) or the 61st year ([[:en:624|624]]). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] ([[:en:718|718]]), during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Emperor Genshō]], an imperial decree was issued to the [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Provincial Governor]] designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ([[:en:722|722]]).. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], our shrine near the provincial capital ([[:en:Kokufu|Provincial Capital]]) invited the spirit manifestations ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of [[:en:Ten'ō|Ten'ō]] ([[:en:781|781]]) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sarara]], exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ([[:en:1186|1186]]) on May 29, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubun-ji]], the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō era]] ([[:en:1180|1180]]) on October 16: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have, on his way to the [[:en:Battle of Fujigawa|Battle of the Fuji River]], arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hojo Tokimasa]] at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji era]] ([[:en:1185|1185]]) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubun-ji]], according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian Period]], our shrine near the provincial capital ([[:en:Kokufu|Provincial Capital]]) invited the spirit manifestations ([[:en:Bunrei|Enshrinement]]) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of [[:en:Ten'ō|Ten'ō]] ([[:en:781|781]]) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Sarara]], exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ([[:en:1186|1186]]) on May 29, when [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubun-ji]], the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of [[:en:Jishō|Jishō era]] ([[:en:1180|1180]]) on October 16: [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have, on his way to the [[:en:Battle of Fujigawa|Battle of the Fuji River]], arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hojo Tokimasa]] at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji era]] ([[:en:1185|1185]]) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the [[:en:Provincial temple|Kokubun-ji]], according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|WD=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}{{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja Shrine}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} (May 5) |prefecture= {{ill|Oiso|WD=Q385023}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.30694444444445|N|139.27444444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vermilion-painted|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|en|1540|WD=Q6482|lt=1540}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Ōiso|en|Ōiso|WD=Q385023|lt=Oiso Town}}, {{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202849|lt=Naka District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja Shrine}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神幸祭|1=shinkō-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123907414}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東山天皇|1=Higashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313801}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|WD=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|en|594|WD=Q33625|lt=594}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|718|en|718|WD=Q23534|lt=718}}), during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Emperor Genshō}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Provincial Governor}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|en|722|WD=Q23542|lt=722}}).. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Provincial Capital}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|en|Ten'ō|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō}} ({{ill|781|en|781|WD=Q31175|lt=781}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sarara}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|en|1186|WD=Q19730|lt=1186}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō era}} ({{ill|1180|en|1180|WD=Q19702|lt=1180}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of Fujigawa|en|Battle of Fujigawa|WD=Q650507|lt=Battle of the Fuji River}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|WD=Q589811|lt=Hojo Tokimasa}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji era}} ({{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Ōiso|en|Ōiso|WD=Q385023|lt=Oiso Town}}, {{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202849|lt=Naka District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja Shrine}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神幸祭|1=shinkō-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123907414}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東山天皇|1=Higashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313801}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|WD=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|en|594|WD=Q33625|lt=594}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|718|en|718|WD=Q23534|lt=718}}), during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Emperor Genshō}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Provincial Governor}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|en|722|WD=Q23542|lt=722}}).. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Provincial Capital}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|en|Ten'ō|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō}} ({{ill|781|en|781|WD=Q31175|lt=781}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sarara}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|en|1186|WD=Q19730|lt=1186}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō era}} ({{ill|1180|en|1180|WD=Q19702|lt=1180}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of Fujigawa|en|Battle of Fujigawa|WD=Q650507|lt=Battle of the Fuji River}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|WD=Q589811|lt=Hojo Tokimasa}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji era}} ({{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|718|en|718|WD=Q23534|lt=718}}), during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Emperor Genshō}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Provincial Governor}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|en|722|WD=Q23542|lt=722}}).. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Provincial Capital}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|en|Ten'ō|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō}} ({{ill|781|en|781|WD=Q31175|lt=781}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sarara}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|en|1186|WD=Q19730|lt=1186}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō era}} ({{ill|1180|en|1180|WD=Q19702|lt=1180}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of Fujigawa|en|Battle of Fujigawa|WD=Q650507|lt=Battle of the Fuji River}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|WD=Q589811|lt=Hojo Tokimasa}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji era}} ({{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rokusho-jinja, Oiso]][[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Rokusho-jinja, Oiso]][[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Rokusho-jinja, Oiso]][[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Infobox religious building| |Name = Rokusho Shrine (六所神社) |image = File:Rokusho jinja Haiden.jpg |Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202849|lt=Naka District}}{{ill|Ōiso|en|Ōiso|WD=Q385023|lt=Oiso Town}} Kokuhongo 935 |Map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|35|18|25|N|139|16|28|E}}}} |Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|WD=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity
{{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}{{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja Shrine}}
Former {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Founded = Kōshin Year of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} |Main Hall = Five-bay shrine, Ryūzō style |Alternate Name = Yanagida Daimyōjin |Festival Date = First Sunday of September |Sacred Rite = {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} (May 5) |prefecture= {{ill|Oiso|WD=Q385023}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.30694444444445|N|139.27444444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Rokusho-jinja (Oiso), torii.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Rokusho jinja Honden.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Main Hall}}{{small|At the time of construction the entire structure was painted {{ill|Vermilion-painted|ja|朱塗り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vermilion-painted|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|en|1540|WD=Q6482|lt=1540}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Ōiso|en|Ōiso|WD=Q385023|lt=Oiso Town}}, {{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202849|lt=Naka District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja Shrine}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神幸祭|1=shinkō-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123907414}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東山天皇|1=Higashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313801}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|WD=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|en|594|WD=Q33625|lt=594}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|718|en|718|WD=Q23534|lt=718}}), during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Emperor Genshō}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Provincial Governor}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|en|722|WD=Q23542|lt=722}}).. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Provincial Capital}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|en|Ten'ō|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō}} ({{ill|781|en|781|WD=Q31175|lt=781}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sarara}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|en|1186|WD=Q19730|lt=1186}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō era}} ({{ill|1180|en|1180|WD=Q19702|lt=1180}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of Fujigawa|en|Battle of Fujigawa|WD=Q650507|lt=Battle of the Fuji River}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|WD=Q589811|lt=Hojo Tokimasa}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji era}} ({{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

There are five doors to enshrine the deities of the five shrines of Sagami Province. According to the ''Kaigen Sōto-ki'', in the 9th year of the Tenbun era ({{ill|1540|en|1540|WD=Q6482|lt=1540}}) the design of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachiman Shrine}} was used as a model, and the shrine’s {{ill|Ridge Board|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ridge Board|1=Sign on a building's pillar stating the construction date and other info|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8008834}} was taken away; in the Heisei restoration the ridge board could not be found.}}]] [[File:Kamisoriyama.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{center|Kamisoriyama}}{{small|At the end of the road stands {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}}, where the {{ill|Zamondō Ritual|ja|座問答神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zamondō Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is held. Photograph taken near Baba Park.}}]] '''Rokusho Shrine''' (六所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Kokuhongo, {{ill|Ōiso|en|Ōiso|WD=Q385023|lt=Oiso Town}}, {{ill|Naka District, Kanagawa|en|Naka District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202849|lt=Naka District}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Sōja shrine|en|Sōja shrine|WD=Q1107129|lt=Sōja Shrine}} of {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}},{{sfn|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki Kō Shinjuku Rokusho Myōjin}} and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Gōsha Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Every year on May 5, the {{ill|Kokumatsuri|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokumatsuri|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11420919}} is held at the ancient divine territory ({{ill|Divine Territory|ja|神領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Territory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}}) on Kamisoriyama (神揃山【also known as 神集山】), and a {{ill|Mikoshi Procession|ja|神輿渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi Procession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神幸祭|1=shinkō-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123907414}} is received from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as from {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of {{ill|Fifth Shrine|ja|五宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東山天皇|1=Higashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313801}} Rank). == Enshrined Deities == At the site of the original Yanagida Daimyōjin Shrine, the enshrined deities from Sagami Province’s {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya}} through to the Fourth Shrine, as well as the enshrined deity of {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} (of Fifth Shrine Rank), were merged (gasshi), and because six shrines’ deities are enshrined here, it is called “Rokusho Shrine.” * Yanagida Daimyōjin – {{ill|Kushinadahime|en|Kushinadahime|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime}} ・ {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo}} ・ {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} * Ichinomiya: {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|WD=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine}} enshrined deity * Second Shrine: {{ill|Kawakazari Shrine|ja|川勾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawakazari Shrine|1=Kawawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11477816}} enshrined deity * Third Shrine: {{ill|Hihita Shrine|ja|比々多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihita Shrine|1=Hibita Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11547364}} enshrined deity * Fourth Shrine: {{ill|Maetori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maetori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11397865}} enshrined deity * {{ill|Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|ja|平塚八幡宮|1=Hiratsuka Hachimangu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachiman Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiratsuka Hachimangu|qq=}} enshrined deity == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}, migrants from {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|WD=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province}} (present-day eastern {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}) who cleared this land named it {{ill|Yanagida Village|ja|柳田郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagida Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and enshrined the ancestral deities of Izumo – Kushinadahime, Susanoo, and Ōkuninushi – under the title “Yanagida Daimyōjin,” building a shrine on Ishikamidai (Ise Shrine platform) about 1 km northwest of the present site. According to the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi''The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'', held in the Rokusho Shrine collection at Renge-in in Kokuhongo, Oiso Town, is part of the Shintō Taikei compilation (edited by the Shintō Taikei Editorial Committee, March 1980). the founding year is said to be the {{ill|Kōshin|ja|1=Yang Wood Monkey|2=ja|甲申|1=Yang Wood Monkey|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Monkey|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} year of Emperor SujinIt is considered to be either the first year of Emperor Sujin ({{ill|594|en|594|WD=Q33625|lt=594}}) or the 61st year ({{ill|624|en|624|WD=Q33247|lt=624}}). In the ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' a note is attached to the Gengshin year section stating “first year.”. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} ({{ill|718|en|718|WD=Q23534|lt=718}}), during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Emperor Genshō}}, an imperial decree was issued to the {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Provincial Governor}} designating our shrine as the religious center and Sōja of Sagami Province, and accordingly it was relocated to its present site on intercalary April 8 of that yearNote: There is no intercalary April in Yōrō 2 (718). The ''Rokusho Myōjin Engi'' notes “six times” in this section, suggesting the correct date may be intercalary April 8 of Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|en|722|WD=Q23542|lt=722}}).. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian Period}}, our shrine near the provincial capital ({{ill|Kokufu|en|Kokufu|WD=Q1629088|lt=Provincial Capital}}) invited the spirit manifestations ({{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}}) of the enshrined deities of the nation’s five powerful shrines, and came to be called the “Kokufu Rokusho-gu.” According to the shrine tradition, in the first year of {{ill|Ten'ō|en|Ten'ō|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō}} ({{ill|781|en|781|WD=Q31175|lt=781}}) on May 5, during the campaign against barbarians by {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sarara}}, exorcism prayers were performed. In the 2nd year of Bunji ({{ill|1186|en|1186|WD=Q19730|lt=1186}}) on May 29, when {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} ordered the restoration of the Sōja of {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System''Edited by the Medieval Ichinomiya System Research Association, ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'', Iwata Shoten, February 2000. suggests that our shrine likely underwent restoration as well. * In the 4th year of {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō era}} ({{ill|1180|en|1180|WD=Q19702|lt=1180}}) on October 16: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} is said to have, on his way to the {{ill|Battle of Fujigawa|en|Battle of Fujigawa|WD=Q650507|lt=Battle of the Fuji River}}, arrived at “Sagami Kokufu Rokusho-gu” and prayed for victory. * In the 4th year of Jishō (1180) on October 23: An article records that awards were given to 25 persons including {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|WD=Q589811|lt=Hojo Tokimasa}} at Sagami Kokufu; it is said this ceremony was held in our shrine’s divine territory. * In the 1st year of {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji era}} ({{ill|1185|en|1185|WD=Q19729|lt=1185}}) on November 24: An article exists stating that a petition for national peace was submitted to 15 temples and 11 shrines in Sagami Province, including our shrine. * In the 2nd year of Bunji (1186) on May 29: When Minamoto no Yoritomo ordered the restoration of the {{ill|Tōkaidō|ja|Tōkaidō (region)|lt=Tōkaidō|lt_ja=Tōkaidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Sōja and the {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Kokubun-ji}}, according to the study ''A Fundamental Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System'' it is highly likely that our shrine also received restoration ==References== {{reflist}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{translated page|ja|六所神社 (大磯町)|qq=Rokusho Shrine (Ōiso)|qq=ja|qq=六所神社 (大磯町)|lt=Rokusho Shrine|version=101725789|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:六所神社 (大磯町)]] {{Commons category|Rokusho-jinja, Oiso}} {{wikidata link|Q11392352}} [[Category:Buildings in Oiso Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ōiso]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
